en.name: Base excess in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Base excess (BE) is a marker of the metabolic component of acid-base balance, computed by blood gas analysers from pH, PCO2 and haemoglobin. Routine values lie within ±2 mmol/L; negative values mean metabolic acidosis, positive values metabolic alkalosis [1, 2]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, expressed in mmol/L. **Note: arterial vs venous.** BE is constructed to be insensitive to the venous-to-arterial PCO2 difference, so arterial, venous, mixed venous and central venous BE are clinically very close and used interchangeably (except in shock or local hypoperfusion). This concept set covers all common blood specimens. **Note: BE vs standard BE.** LOINC distinguishes `Base excess` (in vivo) from `Base excess standard` (with haemoglobin normalised to 5 g/dL). The two are produced together by modern blood gas analysers and differ by 1-2 mmol/L on average. Both are included here; analyses that need to distinguish them must use the LOINC code, not the value. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on six LOINC Hierarchy concepts (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), each expanded to include all their descendants. The set resolves to 11 LOINC "Lab Test" concepts. ### Whole blood - *3012501 / 11555-0 — Base excess in Blood by calculation (LOINC)*: generic blood specimen. ### Arterial blood - *3003396 / 1925-7 — Base excess in Arterial blood by calculation (LOINC)*: most widely used. - *3021421 / 19235-1 — Base excess standard in Arterial blood by calculation (LOINC)*: standardised form. ### Venous blood - *3002032 / 1927-3 — Base excess in Venous blood by calculation (LOINC)* - *3027215 / 19237-7 — Base excess standard in Venous blood by calculation (LOINC)* ### Mixed venous blood - *3023001 / 19234-4 — Base excess in Mixed venous blood by calculation (LOINC)* - *3010329 / 19238-5 — Base excess standard in Mixed venous blood by calculation (LOINC)* ### Central venous blood - *1616922 / 97546-6 — Base excess in Central venous blood by calculation (LOINC)* - *1616406 / 97547-4 — Base excess standard in Central venous blood by calculation (LOINC)* ### Capillary blood - *3003129 / 1926-5 — Base excess in Capillary blood by calculation (LOINC)* - *3026613 / 19236-9 — Base excess standard in Capillary blood by calculation (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits the following LOINC concepts: - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. - **Base excess.100% oxygenated variants**: special calculation under hyperoxic assumption, rarely used outside research. - **Extracellular fluid, nonbiological fluid specimens**: out of scope. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a base excess value without specifying specimen: `3003396 / 1925-7 — Base excess in Arterial blood by calculation`, by far the most common. - **Source reports "BE" or "BE(B)" without distinguishing standard vs in vivo**: in clinical practice this is treated as in vivo (Siggaard-Andersen) BE; map to the `Base excess in X blood` LOINC code rather than the `Base excess standard` one. - **Source explicitly reports SBE (standard base excess)**: map to the matching `Base excess standard in X blood` LOINC code. - **Do not confuse with**: bicarbonate (separate concept set, "Bicarbonate in blood"), anion gap, strong ion difference (different acid-base parameters). ## References [1] Siggaard-Andersen O. The Van Slyke equation. *Scand J Clin Lab Invest Suppl*. 1977;146:15-20. Available: https://doi.org/10.3109/00365517709098927 [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 1925-7 — Base excess in Arterial blood by calculation. Available: https://loinc.org/1925-7/
